function: dameraulevenshtein(s1, s2, int id, int r, int t)
sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), ip_address VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_data (name, ip_address) VALUES ('monetdb', '192.168.1.1/24'), ('monetdb_test', '192.168.2.5/24'), ('test_data', NULL);
SELECT dameraulevenshtein(name, (SELECT name FROM test_data WHERE id = 1), 1, 1, 1) AS distance FROM test_data WHERE ip_address IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ip_address, GROUP_CONCAT(dameraulevenshtein(name, 'test', 1, 1, 1)) AS edit_distances FROM test_data GROUP BY ip_address HAVING COUNT(name) > 1;
SELECT id, inet(ip_address) AS inet_address FROM test_data WHERE inet(ip_address) IS NOT NULL AND dameraulevenshtein(name, 'fake_name', 1, 1, 1) < 3;
SELECT A.id, A.name, A.ip_address FROM test_data A JOIN (SELECT id, dameraulevenshtein(name, 'monetdb', 1, 1, 1) AS distance FROM test_data) B ON A.id = B.id AND B.distance < 5;
SELECT uname, inet(invalid_ip) AS converted INET FROM (SELECT 'user1' AS uname, 'invalid_ip_string' AS invalid_ip) AS subquery WHERE converted INET IS NULL;
SELECT dameraulevenshtein(name, 'empty', 1, 1, 1) FROM test_data WHERE name = '' OR name IS NULL;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE inet(ip_address) IS NOT NULL) AS valid_ips, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE ip_address IS NULL) AS null_ips;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT name, inet(ip_address) AS inet_address FROM test_data WHERE ip_address IS NOT NULL;
